options.explanation: The verb phrase “have been” is the present perfect continuous tense of “to be”. It is used to indicate that an action that started in the indefinite past has come to completion, or is still in progress, at the present. From the given sentence, it can be inferred that “the road is wet” in the present because of ‘rain’ that occurred in the past but evidence of it is seen in the present. Thus, choosing “have been” to complete the given sentence would be most appropriate.

The verb “had” is the past tense and past participle form of the base verb “have”. Past tense is used to describe a completed action (Example: It had my favorite toppings.). Additionally, to form the past perfect, use had and the past participle of a verb in one part of the sentence. The past perfect is used when two events happened in the past, with one past action having occurred even before the other past action.
The verb “has” is a third person singular present indicative of “have”. As an auxiliary verb, “has” is used in present perfect tense.

The verb “have” is an irregular verb (the verb can be used as a main verb or as an auxiliary verb). As an auxiliary verb, “have” is used to make the present perfect tenses of main verbs.The present perfect tense is a tense used in present to indicate the action that has taken place at some specific time. It uses the auxiliary verb “have” and past participle for the main verb i.e. verb + ed.

questions.explanation: The tense used here is present perfect tense so the correct helping verb to use here should be "have been".

questions.text: Demonstrate the correct use of the subject-verb agreement:Some of the books on the table _ to me.

options.explanation: The correct answer is "Some of the books on the table belong to me."The subject of the sentence is "some of the books", which is plural. Therefore, the verb "belong" must also be plural.
"Belonging" is a gerund, which is a verb form that functions as a noun. Gerunds are always singular, so the sentence "Some of the books on the table belonging to me" is not grammatically correct.

"Belongs" is the third-person singular form of the verb "to belong". The third-person singular is used when the subject of the sentence is singular. In this case, the subject is "some of the books", which is plural. Therefore, the verb "belongs" is not grammatically correct.

"Was belong" is the past tense of the verb "to belong". The past tense is used to describe actions that happened in the past. In this case, the sentence is describing the current state of the books, so the past tense is not appropriate.
